title: Wiremock fixture tests cite specific RFC section as oracle and assert concrete field values
description: Every test in methods_test.rs asserts against a value from the hand-written fixture (e.g., assert_eq\!(result.old_state, 'rp-state-001')) rather than just asserting deserialization succeeded. Each fixture file is the independent oracle; each test doc-comment names the RFC section that defines that fixture's shape. A future contributor might weaken assertions to just .is_ok() checks to avoid maintaining fixture values — that would test only that parsing does not panic, not that fields are correctly mapped. The concrete assertions are exactly what detects a field rename or camelCase mapping bug. The fixtures must stay hand-written (not generated from the code under test) per the project's independent-oracle test policy.
